Pain originating in the orofacial region, which includes the mouth and face.
Orofacial pain caused by lesions or disorders affecting one or more teeth or their immediately surrounding and supporting structures: the tooth pulp, periodontium and gingivae.
Dental pain caused by a lesion or disorder involving the tooth pulp.
Orofacial pain involving the masticatory muscles.
Orofacial pain involving the temporomandibular joint (TMJ).
Dental pain caused by a lesion or disorder involving the periodontium.

synonyms.val: periodontal pain due to local non-inflammatory cause

definition.val: Dental pain caused by a lesion or disorder involving the gingival tissues.
Dental pain due to a lesion or disorder involving the jaw bone tissue.
Pulpal pain due to hypersensitivity occurring in clinically normal pulp.
A disposition to decreased tolerance to sensory stimuli that triggers emotional or physical distress.
A tooth fracture involving a crack in the enamel which may or may not involve exposure of dentin. A crack typically appears as a line on the surface of the tooth.
Sensory hypersensitivity involving a tooth.

nodes.lbl: sensory hypersensitivity due to crack in enamel

definition.val: Dental sensory hypersensitivity related a crack in the enamel of a tooth.

nodes.lbl: pulpal pain due to crack in enamel

definition.val: Pulpal pain due to hypersensitivity resulting from a crack or incomplete fracture of the affected tooth, involving the enamel with at least one of the following: sharp pain upon biting, pain on release of occlusal biting pressure or external application of force, or cold hypersensitivity.

nodes.lbl: dentin hypersensitivity due to exposed dentin

synonyms.val: dentine hypersensitivity due to exposed dentine

nodes.lbl: pulpal pain due to exposed dentin

nodes.lbl: dentin hypersensitivity due to tooth wear or abrasion

Temporomandibular pain localized to the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), occurring at rest or during jaw movement or palpation, with no known causative disorder. The diagnosis corresponds fully to the DC/TMD diagnosis temporomandibular joint pain.

Primary temporomandibular pain with onset more than 3 months.

Pain localized to the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) and caused by another identified disorder, such as inflammation (due, for example, to trauma, infection, crystal deposition or autoimmune disorder), sensitization of the tissues, structural changes (such as osteoarthrosis, disc displacement or subluxation) or injury.

Orofacial pain experienced as unilateral brief electric shock-like pains, abrupt in onset and termination, limited to the distribution of one or more divisions of the trigeminal nerve and triggered by innocuous stimuli.

comments: Trigeminal neuralgic pain may develop without apparent cause or be a result of another disorder. Additionally, there may or may not be concomitant continuous pain of moderate intensity within the affected division(s).

synonyms.val: trigeminal neuralgia attributed to other cause

definition.val: Trigeminal neuralgia developing without apparent cause other than neurovascular compression.
Trigeminal neuralgia caused by an underlying disease. Clinical examination shows sensory changes in a substantial percentage of these patients.

nodes.lbl: pain due to lesion or disease of the glossopharyngeal nerve

definition.val: Orofacial pain attributed to lesion or disease of the glossopharyngeal nerve.

A disorder characterized by unilateral brief stabbing pain, abrupt in onset and termination, in the distributions not only of the glossopharyngeal nerve but also of the auricular and pharyngeal branches of the vagus nerve. Pain is experienced in the ear, base of the tongue, tonsillar fossa and/or beneath the angle of the jaw. It is commonly provoked by swallowing, talking or coughing and may remit and relapse in the fashion of Trigeminal neuralgia.
Glossopharyngeal neuralgia with recurrent paroxysms of unilateral pain with the demonstration of magnetic resonance imaging.
Glossopharyngeal neuralgia caused by an underlying disease.
Glossopharyngeal neuralgia with recurrent paroxysms of unilateral pain without neurovascular compression or an underlying disease know to cause secondary glossopharyngeal neuralgia.

synonyms.val: periodontal pain due to unspecified systemic disorder

nodes.lbl: periodontal pain due to genetic disorder

definition.val: Periodontal pain due to periodontitis as a manifestion of a systematic genetic disorder. Genetic disorders which can result in periodontitis include familial and cyclic neutropenia, Down syndrome, leukocyte adhesion deficiency syndromes, Papillon–Lefèvre syndrome, Chediak–Higashi syndrome, histiocytosis syndromes, glycogen storage disease, infantile genetic agranulocytosis, Cohen syndrome, Ehler–Danlos syndrome (types IV and VIII), and hypophosphatasia.

nodes.lbl: hematological disorder due to infectious disorder

definition.val: A hematological disorder caused by an infectious disorder.

comments: The disorder might arise from an infection, but is not necessarily an infection.

nodes.lbl: periodontal pain due to haematological disorder

definition.val: Periodontal pain due to periodontitis as a manifestation of a systematic haematological disorder. Haematological disorders which can result in periodontitis include acquired neutropenia and leukaemia.

nodes.lbl: periodontal pain due to periodontal abscess

An infectious disorder occuring within the periodontium, characterized by a collection of pus.
A disorder characterized by the presence of malignant cells.

A periodontal disorder resulting in inflammation surronding a dental implant.
Peri-implantitis resulting from an infectious disorder.

nodes.lbl: periodontal pain due to infective peri-implantitis

definition.val: Periodontal paind due to periodontitis, with the exception that it involves an implant and not a natural tooth, resulitng from a peri-implant infection with casuation from anatomical, functional, or temporal association.

nodes.lbl: periodontal pain due to apical and marginal periodontitis resulting from combined endodontic infection and periodontal disease

nodes.lbl: oral mucosal inflammation due to non-iatrogenic injury

definition.val: Oral mucosal inflammation due to an injury that is non-iatrogenic.

nodes.lbl: oral mucosal pain due to non-iatrogenic injury

definition.val: Oral mucosal pain due to oral mucosal inflammation resulting from non-iatrogenic injury.

definition.val: Oral mucosal inflammation due to injury resulting from radiation or chemotherapy.

nodes.lbl: oral mucosal pain due to radiation or chemotherapy

definition.val: Oral mucosal pain due to injury from radiation or chemotherapy

nodes.lbl: oral mucosal pain due to malignant lesion

definition.val: Oral mucoal pain due to meoplasia of the oral mucosa.

A salivary gland disorder occuring within the parotid glands.

nodes.lbl: salivary gland pain due to recurrent juvenile parotitis

definition.val: Salivary gland pain due to recurrent juvenile parotitis.

A disorder characterized by a non-cancerous growth.

nodes.lbl: jaw bone pain due to local benign lesion

definition.val: Jaw bone pain that results from a local benign lesion.
A malignant lesion that is confined to where the malignant cells began.

nodes.lbl: jaw bone pain due to local malignancy

definition.val: Jaw bone pain due to a primary malignant lesion.
A malignant lesion that has spread from where teh malignant cells first began.

nodes.lbl: jaw bone pain due to remote malignancy

definition.val: Jaw bone pain due to a remote malignant lesion.

nodes.lbl: jaw bone pain due to therapy

definition.val: Jaw bone pain due to a theraputic intervention that has resulted in a lesion.
Pain in masticatory muscles, with or without functional impairment, not attributable to another disorder.

Mild to moderate levels of deep aching or pressing pain in the masticatory muscles, occurring episodically or unremittingly, often associated with functional impairment such as perceived difficulties in moving the lower jaw, chewing and/or yawning, etc., and with onset within the last 3 months.

Mild to moderate levels of deep aching or pressing pain in the masticatory muscles, occurring episodically or unremittingly, often associated with functional impairment such as perceived difficulties in moving the lower jaw, chewing and/or yawning, etc., and with onset more than 3 months ago. It is often associated with psychosocial distress.

nodes.lbl: chronic infrequent primary myofascial orofacial pain

definition.val: Chronic primary myofascial orofacial pain occurring on less than one day a month.

nodes.lbl: chronic frequent primary myofascial orofacial pain

definition.val: Chronic primary myofascial pain occurring 1-14 days a month for more than 3 months.
Chronic pain that occurs 1-14 days a month.
Chronic pain that occurs less than one day a month.

nodes.lbl: chronic frequent pain with pain referral

definition.val: Chronic frequent pain that occurs beyond the affected site.

nodes.lbl: chronic frequent pain without pain referral

definition.val: Chronic frequent pain that s localized to the affected area.

nodes.lbl: chronic frequent primary myofascial orofacial pain without pain referral

definition.val: Chronic frequent primary myofascial pain with no report of pain at a site beyond the boundary of the muscle (temporalis or masseter) being palpated.

nodes.lbl: chronic frequent primary myofascial orofacial pain with pain referral

definition.val: Chronic frequent primary myofascial pain with a report of pain at a site beyond the boudary of the muscle being palpated.
Chronic pain that occurs more than 15 days a month for more than 4 months.
